items.q: What is required to induce an emf in a coil using a magnet?
items.correct: A changing magnetic flux linkage through the coil — the magnet (or coil) must be moving or the field must be changing.
items.misconception: Just having a magnet near the coil induces an emf, even when both are stationary.
items.random_wrong: A current in the coil is what induces an emf in the magnet.
items.why: Students remember 'magnet + coil = electricity' but miss that the field must be changing for an emf to be induced (Faraday's law).

items.q: In a collision between two objects, what quantity is always conserved (in a closed system)?
items.correct: Total momentum is always conserved; kinetic energy is only conserved in elastic collisions.
items.misconception: Kinetic energy is always conserved in collisions because energy is conserved.
items.random_wrong: Velocity is always conserved in any collision.
items.why: Students conflate momentum conservation with energy conservation; they overlook that energy can become heat/sound in inelastic collisions.

items.q: What produces a magnetic field around a wire?
items.correct: A moving electric charge — i.e. an electric current.
items.misconception: The metal in the wire is naturally magnetic.
items.random_wrong: The voltage across the wire creates magnetism.
items.why: Students think magnetism is intrinsic to the wire material rather than a consequence of charge motion.
The answer examiners want is: Axial tilt. “Distance from the Sun” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.correct: Moon is between Earth and Sun
items.misconception: Earth is between Sun and Moon
items.random_wrong: Sun is between Earth and Moon
items.why: It's Moon is between Earth and Sun. “Earth is between Sun and Moon” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What is Earth's axial tilt in degrees (rounded)?
items.why: Exact answer: 23.5. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
